title: Factor analysis of mixed (numeric + categorical) data
summary: FAMD in the FactoMineR tradition: standardise numerics, disjunctively code categoricals with the (I_k - p_k)/sqrt(p_k) transform, and run SVD on the concatenated matrix. Emits per-batch dimension summaries plus per-variable and per-level coordinate/contribution/cos² tables.
description: For each batch and each n_factors in [min_count, max_count]: complete-case filter, split into numeric and qualitative columns, drop any variable with zero variance or a single-level factor, standardise numerics with n-1 SD, disjunctive code categoricals as (indicator - proportion)/sqrt(proportion), horizontally concatenate, and compute thin SVD of X/sqrt(n). Dimension coordinates are σ·V, contributions are V²·100 (%), cos² is coord²/total per item. Per-variable rows aggregate per-level contributions (categorical) or take the single quantitative row directly. Sheet names follow `<batch>_k<n>_sum`, `<batch>_k<n>_vars`, `<batch>_k<n>_cats` (only emitted when categorical levels exist), truncated to Excel's 31-char limit.
usage:
[["factor-analysis::famd"]]
input = "./data.sav"
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "famd1"
min_count = 2
max_count = 5

meta = [
  { match = "^q\\d+$", batch = "attitudes", type = "ordinal" },
  { variable = "gender", batch = "demographics", type = "categorical" },
]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the input file. CSV or SPSS .sav/.zsav; extension chooses the reader.

arguments.name: meta
arguments.description: Either a meta CSV path or an inline TOML list of row/regex entries. Column `type` drives encoding: numeric is standardised, dichotomous/ordinal/categorical are disjunctively coded. See the fa help page for full inline-meta semantics.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run; no '..', '/' or '\\'.

arguments.name: batches
arguments.description: Subset of batches to analyse. Empty = one FAMD per batch, or a single `all` batch if meta has no batch column.

arguments.name: min_count
arguments.description: Smallest number of dimensions per batch.

arguments.name: max_count
arguments.description: Largest number of dimensions per batch.

arguments.name: na_action
arguments.description: complete_cases (listwise) or fail (abort on NA). FAMD is always listwise per batch.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether CSV inputs have a header row.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of worker threads for the batch × n_factors fan-out. `1` (default) runs serially. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

arguments.name: output_format
arguments.description: Override stdout format: human, protocol, or quiet.

notes: Variables with zero variance (numeric) or a single level (categorical) are dropped from a batch with a warning.
`strongest_dim` / `strongest_abs_coord` identify the axis on which each variable or level loads most heavily.

artifacts.title: Results workbook (one file, two or three sheets per group)
artifacts.description: Every group produces a summary sheet of eigenvalues and variance explained, plus a per-variable sheet of coordinates and contributions. Groups that contain any categorical columns also get a per-level sheet showing how each individual level of each categorical variable sits on the dimensions.

artifacts.title: Eigenvalue summary (one per group)
artifacts.description: Like the corresponding FA sheet: one block of rows per factor-count solution. Compare eigenvalues across solutions to decide how many dimensions to keep.

fields.name: n_factors
fields.description: How many dimensions were extracted for this block. The sheet contains blocks for every value from `min_count` to `max_count`.

fields.name: n_obs
fields.description: Sample size after listwise dropping for this group (respondents with any missing value in the group's columns are excluded).

fields.name: dim
fields.description: The dimension index within this block (1 = first dimension, 2 = second, …). There are `n_factors` rows per block.

fields.name: eigenvalue
fields.description: How much of the total variance this dimension captures, in raw units. Larger = more important.

fields.name: prop
fields.description: Proportion of total variance explained by this dimension.

fields.name: cumulative
fields.description: Running total of `prop` down the block. Tells you how much variance your top-k dimensions explain jointly.

artifacts.title: Per-variable coordinates, contributions, and cos² (one per group)
artifacts.description: One row per (factor-count solution, variable). This is the main interpretive sheet: each variable's coordinate on each dimension (where it sits), its contribution (how much it defines the dimension), and its cos² (how well the dimensions capture it). Numeric variables get a single row; categorical variables also appear here, but their level-by-level detail is on the `_cats` sheet.

fields.name: n_factors
fields.description: The factor-count block this row belongs to.

fields.name: variable
fields.description: The column name in the input data.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Human-readable label from SPSS, the meta file, or the column name as a fallback.

fields.name: is_categorical
fields.description: `true` if the variable is categorical (indicator-coded) or `false` if it is numeric (standardised).

fields.name: coord_dim1 … coord_dim<max_k>
fields.description: Coordinate on each dimension — where the variable sits along that axis. Values far from zero (positive or negative) mean the variable is strongly associated with that dimension. Columns widen to the largest factor count in the sheet; rows from smaller solutions leave higher-dim columns blank.

fields.name: contrib_dim1 … contrib_dim<max_k>
fields.description: Contribution to each dimension, as a percentage. Sums to 100% down each dimension column within a block. A handful of high-contribution variables 'define' the dimension.

fields.name: cos2_dim1 … cos2_dim<max_k>
fields.description: Quality of representation — how well each dimension captures this variable. Range 0–1; a variable with low cos² on every extracted dimension is poorly placed and its coordinates should be read with caution.

fields.name: strongest_dim
fields.description: The dimension index on which this variable loads most strongly (by absolute coordinate). Useful as a secondary sort when exploring the sheet.

fields.name: strongest_abs_coord
fields.description: The absolute coordinate value on that strongest dimension. Quick gauge of how prominent the variable is anywhere in the solution.

artifacts.path: <batch>_cats sheet (only when the group contains categoricals)
artifacts.title: Per-level detail for categorical variables
artifacts.description: One row per (factor-count solution, variable, level). This is where you answer 'which levels of which categoricals are pulling which dimension?'. Numeric variables do not appear on this sheet. Use this alongside `_vars` when naming the dimensions.

fields.name: n_factors
fields.description: The factor-count block this row belongs to.

fields.name: variable
fields.description: The categorical variable's column name.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Human-readable label for the variable.

fields.name: level
fields.description: The specific level (category value) this row is about — e.g. `Male`, `High purchaser`, `Agree strongly`.

fields.name: coord_dim1 … coord_dim<max_k>
fields.description: Where this specific level sits on each dimension. Levels of the same variable can sit very differently — that's the whole point of analysing them separately.

fields.name: contrib_dim1 … contrib_dim<max_k>
fields.description: Contribution of this specific level to each dimension, as a percentage.

fields.name: cos2_dim1 … cos2_dim<max_k>
fields.description: Quality of representation for this level. Low cos² = this level is not well captured by the extracted dimensions.

fields.name: strongest_dim
fields.description: The dimension this level loads most strongly on.

fields.name: strongest_abs_coord
fields.description: Absolute coordinate on that strongest dimension.
